_comment: Imaging report extraction: X-ray, USG/Doppler, CT, MRI, echo, mammogram, TVS. Another config file, not new parsing code.
_why_two_kinds_of_row: An imaging report carries two different things and they are not interchangeable. MEASUREMENTS are numbers with units (ejection fraction 55 %, LVIDd 4.8 cm, a 18 mm follicle, 70 % stenosis) -- they belong in a column, and they are the only part that can be TRENDED: 'what has his EF done over five years' is a question this system should answer. FINDINGS are prose, and prose is where the clinical meaning lives -- 'mild concentric LVH, no regional wall motion abnormality'. Store the numbers as numbers and the prose as prose. Flattening the numbers into the prose loses the trend; summarising the prose into a number loses the medicine.
_impression_is_the_document: If only one field of an imaging report survived, it would be the impression. It is the radiologist's own conclusion, and it is what a doctor reads first. It is copied verbatim and never summarised.
prompt:
You are a precise medical data transcription assistant reading an IMAGING report (X-ray, ultrasound, Doppler, CT, MRI, echocardiogram, mammogram, or a transvaginal/follicular scan).

Copy everything EXACTLY as printed. Do NOT reformat, convert units, round, compute, translate, summarise, abbreviate or normalise anything. TRANSCRIBE, NEVER INTERPRET. If the report prints "55-60%", write "55-60%", not "57". If it prints "1.2 x 0.8 cm", write "1.2 x 0.8 cm". If it prints "No e/o DVT", write "No e/o DVT" -- do not expand the abbreviation. A value or a phrase you alter is a value or a phrase that is wrong.

Do not add a diagnosis, a severity, or a recommendation that the report does not print. Do not resolve a contradiction. Do not fill a gap.

Return a JSON object with exactly four keys: "patient", "study", "measurements", "findings".

"patient": what the report itself prints about who it belongs to. This is an independent cross-check, so read it from the report -- never infer it.
- "name": the patient name exactly as printed. Empty string if absent.
- "age": as printed. Empty string if absent.
- "sex": as printed. Empty string if absent.
- "collected_at": the date the STUDY was performed, as printed (not the report/print date). Empty string if absent.
- "lab": the hospital, imaging centre or radiology department as printed. Empty string if absent.

"study": what was imaged and how.
- "modality": as printed, e.g. "CT", "MRI", "USG", "X-RAY", "2D ECHO", "DOPPLER", "MAMMOGRAM". Empty string if not stated.
- "name": the full study title copied verbatim, e.g. "CT CORONARY ANGIOGRAPHY", "2D ECHOCARDIOGRAPHY WITH COLOUR DOPPLER", "USG WHOLE ABDOMEN", "TRANSVAGINAL SONOGRAPHY".
- "body_part": the region examined, as printed. Empty string if not stated.
- "contrast": exactly as stated, e.g. "with IV contrast", "non-contrast", "plain". Empty string if not stated.
- "technique": any technique/protocol line, verbatim. Empty string if absent.
- "comparison": any prior study it is compared against, verbatim. Empty string if absent.

"measurements": an array, one object per NUMERIC measurement actually printed. A number with a unit, a percentage, or a dimension. Do not invent one, and do not omit one. If the report prints no measurements, return an empty array.
- "section": the heading this measurement sat under, copied verbatim (e.g. "LEFT VENTRICLE", "AORTIC VALVE", "RIGHT OVARY", "LIVER"). Empty string if it sits under no heading. This matters: the same name means different things in different sections -- a "diameter" under RIGHT OVARY is not a "diameter" under AORTIC ROOT.
- "name": what the measurement is called, copied verbatim (e.g. "Ejection Fraction (EF)", "LVIDd", "Endometrial thickness", "Follicle").
- "value": the number copied verbatim, with no units (e.g. "55", "4.8", "1.2 x 0.8", "55-60").
- "unit": the unit copied verbatim (e.g. "%", "cm", "mm", "cm/s", "ml"). Empty string if none is printed. Copy the unit that is PRINTED -- never the unit you expect.
- "reference_range": the normal range copied verbatim if printed. Empty string otherwise.

"findings": an array, one object per prose finding. This is the body of the report. Copy the sentences as they are written.
- "section": the heading it sat under, copied verbatim (e.g. "LIVER", "LEFT VENTRICLE", "IMPRESSION", "CONCLUSION"). Empty string if none.
- "text": the finding copied VERBATIM, word for word. Do not compress it. Do not merge two findings into one. Do not split one across two.
- "is_impression": true ONLY for the report's own IMPRESSION / CONCLUSION / OPINION / SUMMARY section. false for a descriptive finding. If the report has no such section, no object has it true -- do not promote a descriptive finding to an impression, and do not write one yourself.

Ignore page headers, footers, disclaimers, signatures, and the radiologist's registration number. If the page is not an imaging report, return empty arrays.
_validation_comment: Enforced in code, not by the model. Nothing here is a suggestion to the LLM.

validation._prose_coverage_comment: A number is corroborated by an exact substring match against the independent reading. Prose cannot be: one garbled character anywhere in a 40-word paragraph breaks the substring, and Tesseract garbles something in nearly every paragraph -- so an exact test would send every report to review, which is the same as not reading them. Instead, what fraction of the finding's content words did the oracle also see? A TRANSCRIBED impression scores near 1.0 even through bad OCR. An INVENTED one scores near 0.0: the words are not on the page, and OCR noise does not put them there.
validation._prose_coverage_threshold_why: 0.6, not 0.9. The oracle for most of these scans is Tesseract on an image, and it drops words. Set it too high and honest transcriptions are quarantined until the review queue is useless and gets ignored -- which is how a safety feature becomes a nuisance that someone turns off.
